task_completion.description: Load the MRI scan and the existing segmentation into 3D Slicer
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D Slicer application window is visible, showing slice views (axial, sagittal, coronal) with a grayscale MRI image and a colored segmentation overlay loaded.
The 'Segment Editor' module is selected in the module dropdown menu or visible in the left sidebar panel of 3D Slicer.

task_completion.description: Use editing tools to correct segmentation errors
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screenshots show active use of editing tools (e.g., Paint, Erase, Draw) within the Segment Editor. A brush cursor (circle) may be visible on the slice views, or the segmentation mask shape changes between frames.

task_completion.description: Save the corrected segmentation to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Data' or 'Export' dialog is visible, showing the filename 'corrected_segmentation.nii.gz' being saved to the directory '~/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS/'.

task_completion.description: Create and save the QC report JSON file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, VS Code) is open showing JSON content describing segmentation errors, or a save dialog is visible for the file 'qc_report.json' in the correct directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the 3D Slicer GUI for segmentation
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen interacting with the 3D Slicer interface buttons and slice views, not solely running a python script in a terminal to process the images.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not simply copy a ground truth file
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent does not use terminal commands (cp, mv) to move a file from a hidden or system directory to the output path without opening Slicer.

integrity.description: Verify the report was created based on observation
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen typing or pasting generated text into the report file, rather than copying a pre-existing report file.
